scenario: Celery task with @shared_task. Skill must invoke synchronously via task.apply() rather than booting Redis broker + celery worker process.
expected_behaviors: Skill detects shape as 'queue worker' (Celery task)
Skill plans synchronous invocation: send_user_notification.apply(args=[...], kwargs={'dry_run': True})
Skill does NOT attempt to start a Redis broker or 'celery worker' process
Skill captures the Slack POST that the task makes

bugs.description: Skill must use Celery's .apply() (synchronous) — not .delay() (async, needs broker). Booting a broker for QA is wrong; sync invocation is the right v1 answer.
